actionable_feedback: Open attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] in an environment with openpyxl or Excel and confirm every formula cell references the packet fields (R1-R4) and uses the stated min/max normalization constants. Attach a short verifier note (or screenshot) showing a few representative formula cells.

In mitigation_plan.md and in-sheet (or a new sheet) add an explicit cell_reference_map: list of cell addresses and the exact Excel formula text used for ctr_norm, retention_norm, contamination_norm, score, score_plus5pct, score_minus5pct, and the expert_review flag.

Resolve and document the O3 sign semantics concretely: state whether O3 reduces the contamination term magnitude, increases it, or changes sign; update workbook formulas and re-run results for both interpretations as a sensitivity subtable.
Document the treatment of retention7 all-zero case explicitly in the memo and workbook (show normalization fallback or constant) and include a short sensitivity note about its effect on results.

Add a computed pre/post remediation table (either as parseable CSV embedded in artifacts or a sheet with computed numeric columns) so that parsers can validate numeric outcomes even if formulas are unreadable in some environments.
Mark semantic correctness claims in mitigation_plan.md and manifest as conditional until workbook-level verification is complete; include the verifier confirmation in the revised manifest.

Add simple in-workbook self-checks (assertion cells) that compare computed scores to the example numeric results in the memo (R1-R4) and set a PASS/FAIL flag for the workbook consistency test; include the pass/fail outcome in actual_outputs.json.
artifact_specific_comments: mitigation_plan.md: Clear and well-structured. Explicitly lists assumptions, scoring formula, sensitivity logic, and remediation cost table. Flags unresolved ambiguities (exploration_pct, O3) correctly but should resolve them or propagate conditionality through recommendations.

feedback_loop_audit.xlsx: Trace indicates the workbook was written by assembling OOXML. The file exists but could not be parsed in the verification environment (openpyxl unavailable), so cell-level formula links and exact formula expressions need verifier confirmation.

actual_outputs.json: Contains a clear manifest and results summary but should include an explicit workbook-verification field after the XLSX is validated (e.g., workbook_parsed: true and verification_notes).
feedback_summary: Deliverables present and memo documents normalization, scoring formula, sensitivity checks, and remediation recommendation. Deterministic artifact-contract precheck passed. However the XLSX binary could not be parsed in the verifier environment (openpyxl unavailable), so workbook cell-level formulas and in-sheet multipliers are unverified. The grader awarded full semantic correctness despite the precheck note that semantic correctness was not evaluated; that claim is unsupported until the workbook is opened and formulas confirmed. Ambiguities flagged in the memo (exploration_pct role and O3 sign semantics) were not fully resolved and therefore some remediation conclusions should remain conditional pending workbook verification.

recommended_verifier_steps: Open attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] in an environment with openpyxl or Excel and confirm each formula cell references the packet fields and uses the stated min/max constants.
Re-run +/-5% sensitivity checks within the workbook to confirm classification stability and the expert_review flag logic.
Verify remediation option arithmetic in the workbook (cost summation and application of multipliers) and confirm that O1+O2 is the budget-feasible best set under declared tie-breakers.
Confirm that the workbook self-check assertion cells (if added) pass and attach their output to the revised manifest.

verifier_issues_summary.detail: The mitigation_plan.md documents formulas and the trace claims an OOXML workbook was written with formulas, but the XLSX binary preview could not be parsed (No module named 'openpyxl'). Workbook-level claims therefore require manual verification.
verifier_issues_summary.title: Workbook cell-level formulas not verifiable from previews

verifier_issues_summary.detail: A semantic score of 1.0 was reported despite 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated' in the deterministic precheck and the inability to parse the workbook. Do not claim full semantic correctness until workbook-level verification is attached.
verifier_issues_summary.title: Semantic correctness claim not fully supported

verifier_issues_summary.detail: Exploration_pct role and O3 sign semantics remain ambiguous. Recommendations that depend on these interpretations should be marked conditional until resolved.
verifier_issues_summary.title: Ambiguities flagged but impact not fully propagated
